english: Dzl. VS, 3. — 2. = gtód-pa (gtod pa), to face, to front, to look towards, sgo lhó-pyogs-su ston (sgo lho phyogs su ston) the door faces the south Glr. — 3. to point out, to indicate, describe, explain, čé-ba (che ba) the greatness or superiority of a thing Mil.; bú-mo skyé bar gyúr-bar stón-pa yin (bu mo skye bar gyur bar ston pa yin) it indicates that a girl will be born Wdn.; čí-'dra žig (yod) ston dgos (ci 'dra zhig (yod) ston dgos) give me a description of her person Glr.; bstán-par byao (bstan par byao) now I will explain that, frq.; ji-ltar byón-pa bstán-pai leu (ji ltar byon pa bstan pai leu) the chapter describing the arrival; hence to teach, čos (chos) religion; luṅ (lung) v. luṅ (lung). — 4. W. to make one undergo or suffer, to inflict (just as *tóṅ-če* (*tong ce*) to suffer), *mí-la nag stón-pa* (*mi la nag ston pa*) to torture a person, *dug-ṅálstón-pa* (*dug ngalston pa*) to plague, torment, grieve. — 5. W. as a vb. nt., to show one's self, to appear, *'í-ru tan-te yod* (*'i ru tan te yod*) this appears here, this turns up or occurs here. II. sbst. a teacher, frq., lúṅ-ston-pa (lung ston pa) a prophet, v. luṅ (lung); the stón-pa (ston pa) par excellence is Buddha, frq.; — ston-mín (ston min), and tse-mín (tshe min) two false doctrines Glr. 92, 3. (the translation given by Sch. is but an arbitrary one).

feast, banquet (v. also yá-tra (ya tra)), stón-mo bzáṅ-po, čén-po (ston mo bzang po, chen po), a grand, splendid feast Dzl.; bšóm-pa (bshom pa) to prepare, arrange (a feast), byéd-pa (byed pa) to give, hold, celebrate it, also c. dat. in honour of; stón-mo dréṅ-pa (ston mo dreng pa) to serve it up Mil., gyéd-pa (gyed pa) to distribute the dishes, dmáṅs-kyi stón-mo gyéd-pa (dmangs kyi ston mo gyed pa) to distribute of the viands of the table to the common people Mil., zá-ba (za ba) to eat, or partake, of such a festive entertainment Dzl.; stón-mo-yaṅ-sbyin (ston mo yang sbyin) a present of meat, of provisions Glr.; dgá-ston (dga' ston) festive entertainment, frq.; rná-bai dgá-ston (rna bai dga' ston) a feast or treat to one's ears Glr.; čós-ston (chos ston) a religious feast Glr. (might be used for agapê, love-feast, feast of charity); dús-ston (dus ston) a periodical festival, one connected with certain times or periods Tar.; bag-ston (bag ston) wedding-feast, frq.; míṅ-ston (ming ston) feast given at the solemnity, when a name is given to a child; ráb-ston (rab ston) a feast after settling some important business Cs.; btsás-ston (btsas ston) a feast given after the birth of a child; tsógs-ston (tshogs ston) sacrificatory feast; gšid-ston (gshid ston) funeral feast.

pf. bstab (bstab) ((Cs. bstob (bstob)), fut. bstob (bstob) Cs., imp. stob (stob), (causative to ťob-pa (thob pa)?), to put into another's mouth esp. food, to feed; also applied to a mare that shoves the grass to her foal Dzl.; náṅ-tan-gyis stób-pa (nang tan gyis stob pa) to press a person to accept of a dish etc. Dzl.; in a more general sense: láṅ-ste stan stób-par byéd-pa (lang ste stan stob par byed pa) rising to offer one's own seat Stg.; to make a donation Dzl.; also figur.: yo-byád tams-čád-kyis stób-pa (yo byad thams cad kyis stob pa) to provide a person with every thing within one's power Tar.

(-po (-po)) strength, vigour, force, frq.; lús-stobs (lus stobs) bodily, snyíṅ-stobs (snying stobs) mental strength; 'jú-stobs ('ju stobs) digestive power Med.; stóbs-po čé (stobs po che) of great physical strength Dzl.; stóbs-kyis (stobs kyis) by virtue, by means of; stobs-pél-nyams-brtás byéd-pa (stobs spel nyams brtas byed pa) strengthening, nourishing, of food Med.; stóbs-čan (stobs can), stobs-ldán (stobs ldan), strong, robust; stobs-čúṅ (stobs chung), stobs-méd (stobs med), powerless, weak; the five powers of a Buddha v. Burn. II, 430; Köpp. I, 436; the ten powers v. dbaṅ bču (dbang bcu). — stobs-čén (stobs chen) 1. n. of a Lu-king, S.O. — 2. rammer, pile-driver, (or rdob-čén (rdob chen)?) C.

to be lost, to perish, to go astray, bu stór-ro (bu stor ro) a child has been lost Dzl.; lus daṅ srog (lus dang srog) (to lose) one's life Dzl., sems (sems) one's senses, lam (lam) one's way (also fig. to err from true religion Pth.); *tor ma čug* (*tor ma cug*) W. do not lose it, do not drop it, carry it carefully; stór-sa med (stor sa med) it cannot be lost or antiquated Mil. — stór-kuṅ (stor kung) for gtór-kuṅ (gtor kung) drain, gutter Lex.

pf. brtas (brtas), Lex.: lus sems brtas (lus sems brtas), explained by rgyás-pa (rgyas pa), to grow wide, to extend; gen. to grow stout, esp. with nyams (nyams) Dzl.; cf. also the expression for strengthening sub stóbs(-po) (stobs (-po)); also rtas byéd-pa (rtas byed pa) Med.; fig. strong or great: gyód-pa rtas (gyod pa rtas) the greatest, the sincerest repentance Pth.; bág-čags rtás-pa (bag chags rtas pa) high passion Thgy.
